title: rubus laschii
intro: Rubus laschii, also known as the New Zealand black raspberry or New Zealand black thimbleberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family. It is a shrub that produces small, edible black fruits rich in antioxidants and has been used for its medicinal properties. The plant's leaves and roots have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Rubus laschii can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function.
